utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: The format requires each of the first three to include 'with costs' and be clearly about the specified domain. Health_coverage includes a provider, monthly_cost, and features (covers physical/mental implicitly but not explicitly; however it does include costs). Property_coverage includes provider, monthly_cost, and features (home/contents implied; includes costs). Travel_coverage includes provider, monthly_cost, and features (includes costs). Additional_services requires 'any enhancement services with costs'—the plan lists services but no costs, so this is not fully specified. Total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; it includes monthly_total and annual_total (and an extra savings figure), so it is fully specified. Therefore, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three providers: LifeFirst Standard Plan for health, HomeFirst Standard Coverage for property, and InsurePlus Standard Travel Plan for travel. I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage.physical_health, property_protection.home_content, and mobility_coverage.travel_insurance respectively. Their ratings are 8, 7, and 7.
